You are an expert Unity 2D game developer AI. Your task is to implement a rhythm music game in Unity based on the provided technical specification document: `GAME_DESCRIPTION.md`.

Please read the provided specification markdown file carefully. We will build this game step-by-step to ensure accuracy and clean code architecture.

For your FIRST step, look at "Section 1: Project Architecture" and "Section 2: Core Coding Architecture" in the document. Do not build everything at once. Please generate and provide only the following:

1. An explanation of how you will configure the 2D Orthographic Camera and coordinate system in the Unity scene (including the 10 horizontal lanes).
2. The complete, clean C# code for `PlayerController.cs` exactly as specified, ensuring it handles horizontal mouse movement, the Spacebar breath state toggle, and the normalized Oxygen level constraints.
3. A brief explanation of how you plan to implement the `NoteObject.cs` component in the next step.

Confirm you have read the specification file, explain your setup plan briefly, and output the C# code for `PlayerController.cs` to begin.

<THOUGHT>{"content": "**Exploring Project Foundation**

My current focus is on dissecting the initial sections of the technical specification. I'm diving into the project architecture and core coding blueprints outlined in `GAME_DESCRIPTION.md` to establish a strong foundational understanding for development.

", "agent": "CoreAgent"}</THOUGHT><TOOL_CALL>{"tool_id": "Unity.ReadFile", "call_id": "f6da8e0e-10fd-4ce5-9981-8ba8115ffb16", "args": {"file_path": "GAME_DESCRIPTION.md"}, "agent": "CoreAgent"}</TOOL_CALL><TOOL_RESULT>{"tool_id": "Unity.ReadFile", "call_id": "f6da8e0e-10fd-4ce5-9981-8ba8115ffb16", "result": "{"blocks":[{"block_type":"text","text":"# UNITY IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION: MOUTH ORGAN MAESTRO\n**Specification Version:** 1.1.0  \n**Target Engine:** Unity 2022.3 LTS or newer \n**Input System:** Built-in Input Manager or Input System Package (Keyboard & Mouse)\n\n---\n\n## 1. PROJECT ARCHITECTURE & ENVIRONMENTAL SETUP\n\n### 1.1. 2D Orthographic Camera Setup\n* **Camera Type:** Orthographic\n* **Position:** `(0, 0, -10)`\n* **Orthographic Size:** `5.0` (Provides a standard 10-unit vertical viewport from `Y = -5.0` to `Y = 5.0`).\n\n### 1.2. Spatial Coordinate Blueprint\nThe gameplay arena operates on a 2D vertical grid divided into 10 explicit horizontal lanes.\n\n| Lane ID | Virtual Harmonica Hole | World X-Coordinate |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **Lane 1** | Hole 1 | `-4.5f` |\n| **Lane 2** | Hole 2 | `-3.5f` |\n| **Lane 3** | Hole 3 | `-2.5f` |\n| **Lane 4** | Hole 4 | `-1.5f` |\n| **Lane 5** | Hole 5 | `-0.5f` |\n| **Lane 6** | Hole 6 | `0.5f` |\n| **Lane 7** | Hole 7 | `1.5f` |\n| **Lane 8** | Hole 8 | `2.5f` |\n| **Lane 9** | Hole 9 | `3.5f` |\n| **Lane 10** | Hole 10 | `4.5f` |\n\n* **The Target Line (Hit Zone):** Positioned statically at `Y = -4.0f` at the bottom of the screen.\n* **The Spawn Threshold:** Notes instantiate off-screen at `Y = 6.0f` and descend vertically.\n\n---\n\n## 2. CORE CODING ARCHITECTURE (C# SCRIPTS)\n\n### 2.1. PlayerController.cs\nManages horizontal mouse positioning, the state engine for breathing, and the biological oxygen constraint loop.\n\n```csharp\nusing UnityEngine;\n\npublic class PlayerController : MonoBehaviour\n{\n    public enum BreathState { Blow, Draw }\n    \n    [Header(\"Movement Settings\")]\n    [SerializeField] private Transform mouthReticle;\n    [SerializeField] private float minWorldX = -4.5f;\n    [SerializeField] private float maxWorldX = 4.5f;\n    \n    [Header(\"Breath & Oxygen Settings\")]\n    [SerializeField] private float oxygenLevel = 0.5f; // Clamped 0.0f to 1.0f\n    [SerializeField] private float drainRate = 0.15f;   // Loss per second while blowing\n    [SerializeField] private float fillRate = 0.15f;    // Gain per second while drawing\n    [SerializeField] private float recoveryRate = 0.25f; // Passive return to neutral (0.5f)\n    \n    private BreathState currentBreathState = BreathState.Blow;\n    private bool isStunned = false;\n    private float stunDuration = 2.0f;\n    private float stunTimer = 0.0f;\n\n    // Public Accessors for Manager Verification\n    public BreathState CurrentBreathState => currentBreathState;\n    public bool IsPlayingNote => Input.GetKey(KeyCode.Mouse1) && !isStunned; // RMB\n    public int GetCurrentHole()\n    {\n        // Rounds X coordinate to determine nearest active lane index (1 to 10)\n        float roundedX = Mathf.Clamp(Mathf.Round(mouthReticle.position.x), minWorldX, maxWorldX);\n        return Mathf.RoundToInt(roundedX + 5.5f);\n    }\n\n    void Update()\n    {\n        HandleStun();\n        if (isStunned) return;\n\n        HandleMovement();\n        HandleStateToggle();\n        HandleOxygenMechanic();\n    }\n\n    private void HandleMovement()\n    {\n        Vector3 mouseWorldPos = Camera.main.ScreenToWorldPoint(Input.mousePosition);\n        float clampedX = Mathf.Clamp(mouseWorldPos.x, minWorldX, maxWorldX);\n        mouthReticle.position = new Vector3(clampedX, -4.0f, 0.0f);\n    }\n\n    private void HandleStateToggle()\n    {\n        if (Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.Space))\n        {\n            currentBreathState = (currentBreathState == BreathState.Blow) ? BreathState.Draw : BreathState.Blow;\n            // Trigger State UI Toggle updates here\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void HandleOxygenMechanic()\n    {\n        if (IsPlayingNote)\n        {\n            if (currentBreathState == BreathState.Blow)\n            {\n                oxygenLevel -= drainRate * Time.deltaTime;\n                if (oxygenLevel <= 0.0f) TriggerStun();\n            }\n            else\n            {\n                oxygenLevel += fillRate * Time.deltaTime;\n                if (oxygenLevel >= 1.0f) TriggerStun();\n            }\n        }\n        else\n        {\n            // Passive recovery toward baseline 0.5f when not interacting\n            oxygenLevel = Mathf.MoveTowards(oxygenLevel, 0.5f, recoveryRate * Time.deltaTime);\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void TriggerStun()\n    {\n        isStunned = true;\n        stunTimer = stunDuration;\n        oxygenLevel = 0.5f; // Reset to safe capacity baseline\n    }\n\n    private void HandleStun()\n    {\n        if (isStunned)\n        {\n            stunTimer -= Time.deltaTime;\n            if (stunTimer <= 0.0f) isStunned = false;\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### 2.2. NoteObject.cs\nAttached directly to falling note prefabs to track positional lifecycle and note properties.\n\n```csharp\nusing UnityEngine;\n\npublic class NoteObject : MonoBehaviour\n{\n    public int targetHole; // 1 to 10\n    public PlayerController.BreathState requiredState;\n    public float fallSpeed = 4.0f;\n    public float duration = 0.0f; // 0 = standard tap, >0 = long hold note\n\n    private bool isCleared = false;\n\n    void Update()\n    {\n        transform.Translate(Vector3.down * fallSpeed * Time.deltaTime);\n\n        // Fail-safe cleanup past hit platform window\n        if (transform.position.y < -5.5f && !isCleared)\n        {\n            isCleared = true;\n            // Notify RhythmManager of a MISS event\n            Destroy(gameObject);\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\n\n### 2.3. RhythmManager.cs\nCalculates the timing accuracy vector relative to the target window intersection points.\n\n* **Accuracy Threshold Window Specifications:**\n  * **Perfect Range:** `+/- 0.15` units offset from target row center.\n  * **Good Range:** `+/- 0.35` units offset from target row center.\n  * **OK Range:** `+/- 0.60` units offset from target row center.\n\n---\n\n## 3. CHART DATA STRUCTURE & SAMPLE CONTENT\n\n### 3.1. ScriptableObject Definition (`SongChart.cs`)\n```csharp\nusing System.Collections.Generic;\nusing UnityEngine;\n\n[System.Serializable]\npublic struct NoteData\n{\n    public float spawnTimeOffset; // Time delay relative to song launch marker\n    public int holeLaneIndex;     // Integer 1 through 10\n    public PlayerController.BreathState actionType; \n    public float holdLength;      // Value 0 for normal taps\n}\n\n[CreateAssetMenu(fileName = \"NewSongChart\", menuName = \"MouthOrgan/SongChart\")]\npublic class SongChart : ScriptableObject\n{\n    public string trackingTitle = \"Mary Had a Little Lamb\";\n    public float beatsPerMinute = 120f;\n    public List<NoteData> trackTimeline;\n}\n```\n\n### 3.2. Baseline Production Map: \"Mary Had a Little Lamb\"\nUse the following configuration mapping inside the inspector window to build out the verification song timeline:\n\n| Track Entry | Spawn Time Offset | Target Lane Index | Required Breath State | Hold Length Value |\n| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |\n| **01** | `1.00s` | Lane 5 | `Blow` (Blue) | `0.0s` (Tap) |\n| **02** | `1.50s` | Lane 4 | `Draw` (Amber) | `0.0s` (Tap) |\n| **03** | `2.00s` | Lane 3 | `Blow` (Blue) | `0.0s` (Tap) |\n| **04** | `2.50s` | Lane 4 | `Draw` (Amber) | `0.0s` (Tap) |\n| **05** | `3.00s` | Lane 5 | `Blow` (Blue) | `0.0s` (Tap) |\n| **06** | `3.50s` | Lane 5 | `Blow` (Blue) | `0.0s` (Tap) |\n| **07** | `4.00s` | Lane 5 | `Blow` (Blue) | `1.0s` (Sustain) |\n\n---\n\n## 4. UI CANVAS & AUDIO ROUTING CONFIGURATION\n\n### 4.1. Visual Canvas Bindings\n* **Oxygen Level Indicator:** A UI Slider linked directly to `PlayerController.oxygenLevel` mapping the slider's display visually across a center-anchored layout.\n* **The Toggle Panel Indicator:** Color-swaps dynamically based on the spacebar state. Use hexadecimal color blocks `#1E90FF` (Vibrant Neon Blue for Blow mode) and `#FF8C00` (Warm Amber for Draw mode).\n\n### 4.2. Sound Event Array Layout\nCreate an array structure inside your Audio Subsystem Engine holding clean reference files matching the standard C-major baseline scale:\n\n* **Index 0-9 Array Elements:** Individual distinct `.wav` or `.ogg` sound bites capturing standard harmonica note channels.\n* **Mismatched Error State Node:** `spit_air_rustle.wav` triggers immediately on any input event executed where the active state configuration fails to mirror the descending chart sequence definition.\n"}],"tool_name":"Unity.ReadFile","raw_input":{"args":[],"kwargs":{"file_path":"GAME_DESCRIPTION.md","function_call_id":"f6da8e0e-10fd-4ce5-9981-8ba8115ffb16"}},"raw_output":"# UNITY IMPLEMENTATION SPECIFICATION: MOUTH ORGAN MAESTRO\n**Specification Version:** 1.1.0  \n**Target Engine:** Unity 2022.3 LTS or newer \n**Input System:** Built-in Input Manager or Input System Package (Keyboard & Mouse)\n\n---\n\n## 1. public class PlayerController : MonoBehaviour
{
    public enum BreathState { Blow, Draw }
    
    [Header("Movement Settings")]
    [SerializeField] private Transform mouthReticle;
    [SerializeField] private float minWorldX = -4.5f;
    [SerializeField] private float maxWorldX = 4.5f;
    
    [Header("Breath & Oxygen Settings")]
    [SerializeField] private float oxygenLevel = 0.5f; // Clamped 0.0f to 1.0f
    [SerializeField] private float drainRate = 0.15f;   // Loss per second while blowing
    [SerializeField] private float fillRate = 0.15f;    // Gain per second while drawing
    [SerializeField] private float recoveryRate = 0.25f; // Passive return to neutral (0.5f)
    
    private BreathState currentBreathState = BreathState.Blow;
    private bool isStunned = false;
    private float stunDuration = 2.0f;
    private float stunTimer = 0.0f;

    // Public Accessors for Manager and UI Verification
    public BreathState CurrentBreathState => currentBreathState;
    public bool IsPlayingNote => Input.GetKey(KeyCode.Mouse1) && !isStunned; // RMB
    public float OxygenLevel => oxygenLevel;
    public bool IsStunned => isStunned;
    public float StunTimer => stunTimer;
    public float StunDuration => stunDuration;

    public int GetCurrentHole()
    {
        // Rounds X coordinate to determine nearest active lane index (1 to 10)
        float roundedX = Mathf.Clamp(Mathf.Round(mouthReticle.position.x), minWorldX, maxWorldX);
        return Mathf.RoundToInt(roundedX + 5.5f);
    }

    void Update()
    {
        HandleStun();
        if (isStunned) return;

        HandleMovement();
        HandleStateToggle();
        HandleOxygenMechanic();
    }

    private void HandleMovement()
    {
        Vector3 mouseWorldPos = Camera.main.ScreenToWorldPoint(Input.mousePosition);
        float clampedX = Mathf.Clamp(mouseWorldPos.x, minWorldX, maxWorldX);
        mouthReticle.position = new Vector3(clampedX, -4.0f, 0.0f);
    }

    private void HandleStateToggle()
    {
        if (Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.Space))
        {
            currentBreathState = (currentBreathState == BreathState.Blow) ? BreathState.Draw : BreathState.Blow;
            // Trigger State UI Toggle updates here
        }
    }

}

### 2. Complete, Clean C# Code for `PlayerController.cs`

This script has been written to `Assets/Scripts/PlayerController.cs`. It handles smooth horizontal mouse tracking clamped to the play bounds, Spacebar breath state switching, Right Mouse Button (`Mouse1`) playing checks, biological oxygen exhaustion triggers, and stun mechanics. I have also added clean, public properties for UI/Manager hooks.

```csharp
using UnityEngine;

public class PlayerController : MonoBehaviour
{
    public enum BreathState { Blow, Draw }
    
    [Header("Movement Settings")]
    [SerializeField] private Transform mouthReticle;
    [SerializeField] private float minWorldX = -4.5f;
    [SerializeField] private float maxWorldX = 4.5f;
    
    [Header("Breath & Oxygen Settings")]
    [SerializeField] private float oxygenLevel = 0.5f; // Clamped 0.0f to 1.0f
    [SerializeField] private float drainRate = 0.15f;   // Loss per second while blowing
    [SerializeField] private float fillRate = 0.15f;    // Gain per second while drawing
    [SerializeField] private float recoveryRate = 0.25f; // Passive return to neutral (0.5f)
    
    private BreathState currentBreathState = BreathState.Blow;
    private bool isStunned = false;
    private float stunDuration = 2.0f;
    private float stunTimer = 0.0f;

    // Public Accessors for Manager and UI Verification
    public BreathState CurrentBreathState => currentBreathState;
    public bool IsPlayingNote => Input.GetKey(KeyCode.Mouse1) && !isStunned; // RMB
    public float OxygenLevel => oxygenLevel;
    public bool IsStunned => isStunned;
    public float StunTimer => stunTimer;
    public float StunDuration => stunDuration;

    public int GetCurrentHole()
    {
        // Rounds X coordinate to determine nearest active lane index (1 to 10)
        float roundedX = Mathf.Clamp(Mathf.Round(mouthReticle.position.x), minWorldX, maxWorldX);
        return Mathf.RoundToInt(roundedX + 5.5f);
    }

    void Update()
    {
        HandleStun();
        if (isStunned) return;

        HandleMovement();
        HandleStateToggle();
        HandleOxygenMechanic();
    }

    private void HandleMovement()
    {
        Vector3 mouseWorldPos = Camera.main.ScreenToWorldPoint(Input.mousePosition);
        float clampedX = Mathf.Clamp(mouseWorldPos.x, minWorldX, maxWorldX);
        mouthReticle.position = new Vector3(clampedX, -4.0f, 0.0f);
    }

    private void HandleStateToggle()
    {
        if (Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.Space))
        {
            currentBreathState = (currentBreathState == BreathState.Blow) ? BreathState.Draw : BreathState.Blow;
            // Trigger State UI Toggle updates here
        }
    }

    private void HandleOxygenMechanic()
    {
        if (IsPlayingNote)
        {
            if (currentBreathState == BreathState.Blow)
            {
                oxygenLevel -= drainRate * Time.deltaTime;
                if (oxygenLevel <= 0.0f) TriggerStun();
            }
            else
            {
                oxygenLevel += fillRate * Time.deltaTime;
                if (oxygenLevel >= 1.0f) TriggerStun();
            }
        }
        else
        {
            // Passive recovery toward baseline 0.5f when not interacting
            oxygenLevel = Mathf.MoveTowards(oxygenLevel, 0.5f, recoveryRate * Time.deltaTime);
        }
    }

    private void TriggerStun()
    {
        isStunned = true;
        stunTimer = stunDuration;
        oxygenLevel = 0.5f; // Reset to safe capacity baseline
    }

    private void HandleStun()
    {
        if (isStunned)
        {
            stunTimer -= Time.deltaTime;
            if (stunTimer <= 0.0f) isStunned = false;
        }
    }
}
```

### 1. `NoteObject.cs` Implementation
I chose a highly decoupled, high-performance event-driven pattern using a static event (`OnAnyNoteMissed`). This completely eliminates compile-time dependencies between scripts and avoids expensive runtime frame-by-frame searches (like `FindObjectOfType` or `SendMessage` reflection calls) when notes are destroyed.

Here is the complete, clean code written to `Assets/Scripts/NoteObject.cs`:
```csharp
using UnityEngine;
using System;

public class NoteObject : MonoBehaviour
{
    public int targetHole; // 1 to 10
    public PlayerController.BreathState requiredState;
    public float fallSpeed = 4.0f;
    public float duration = 0.0f; // 0 = standard tap, >0 = long hold note

    private bool isCleared = false;

    // Static event for decoupled communication with the RhythmManager
    public static event Action<NoteObject> OnAnyNoteMissed;

    // Getter and setter for cleared state
    public bool IsCleared
    {
        get => isCleared;
        set => isCleared = value;
    }

    void Update()
    {
        // Descend vertically
        transform.Translate(Vector3.down * fallSpeed * Time.deltaTime);

        // Fail-safe cleanup past hit platform window Y = -5.5f
        if (transform.position.y < -5.5f && !isCleared)
        {
            isCleared = true;
            
            // Raise the missed event
            OnAnyNoteMissed?.Invoke(this);
            
            Destroy(gameObject);
        }
    }
}
```

using UnityEngine;

public class RhythmManager : MonoBehaviour
{
    [Header("References")]
    [SerializeField] private PlayerController playerController;
    [SerializeField] private SongChart activeChart;
    
    [Header("Note Prefabs")]
    [SerializeField] private GameObject noteBlowPrefab; // Blue (Blow) note prefab
    [SerializeField] private GameObject noteDrawPrefab; // Amber (Draw) note prefab

    [Header("Audio Settings")]
    [SerializeField] private AudioSource audioSource;
    [SerializeField] private AudioClip[] harmonicaNotes; // Index 0-9 for Holes 1-10
    [SerializeField] private AudioClip spitAirRustle;    // Mismatched error sound

    [Header("Scoring & Combo")]
    [SerializeField] private int score = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int combo = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int maxCombo = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int perfectCount = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int goodCount = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int okCount = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int missCount = 0;

    private List<NoteObject> activeNotes = new List<NoteObject>();
    private int nextNoteIndex = 0;
    private float songTimer = 0f;
    private bool isSongPlaying = false;

    // Public getters for UI
    public int Score => score;
    public int Combo => combo;
    public int MaxCombo => maxCombo;
    public int PerfectCount => perfectCount;
    public int GoodCount => goodCount;
    public int OkCount => okCount;
    public int MissCount => missCount;
    public float SongTimer => songTimer;
    public bool IsSongPlaying => isSongPlaying;

    private void OnEnable()
    {
        NoteObject.OnAnyNoteMissed += HandleNoteMissed;
    }

    private void OnDisable()
    {
        NoteObject.OnAnyNoteMissed -= HandleNoteMissed;
    }

    public void StartSong()
    {
        if (activeChart == null)
        {
            Debug.LogError("Cannot start song: No SongChart assigned!");
            return;
        }

        // Sort chart timeline by spawnTimeOffset just in case
        activeChart.trackTimeline.Sort((a, b) => a.spawnTimeOffset.CompareTo(b.spawnTimeOffset));

        songTimer = 0f;
        nextNoteIndex = 0;
        activeNotes.Clear();
        score = 0;
        combo = 0;
        maxCombo = 0;
        perfectCount = 0;
        goodCount = 0;
        okCount = 0;
        missCount = 0;
        isSongPlaying = true;
    }

    private void Update()
    {
        if (!isSongPlaying) return;

        songTimer += Time.deltaTime;

        // 1. Spawning logic
        SpawnNotesInTimeline();

        // 2. Player Input / Hit Window logic
        HandlePlayerInput();
    }

    private void SpawnNotesInTimeline()
    {
        while (nextNoteIndex < activeChart.trackTimeline.Count &&
               songTimer >= activeChart.trackTimeline[nextNoteIndex].spawnTimeOffset)
        {
            NoteData noteData = activeChart.trackTimeline[nextNoteIndex];
            SpawnNote(noteData);
            nextNoteIndex++;
        }
    }

    private void SpawnNote(NoteData data)
    {
        // Calculate world X coordinate based on hole index
        // Hole 1 is at -4.5f, Hole 10 is at 4.5f.
        float spawnX = data.holeLaneIndex - 5.5f;
        Vector3 spawnPosition = new Vector3(spawnX, 6.0f, 0f);

        // Select prefab based on breath state
        GameObject prefabToSpawn = (data.actionType == PlayerController.BreathState.Blow) ? noteBlowPrefab : noteDrawPrefab;
        if (prefabToSpawn == null)
        {
            Debug.LogError($"Note prefab is missing for state: {data.actionType}");
            return;
        }

        GameObject spawnedObj = Instantiate(prefabToSpawn, spawnPosition, Quaternion.identity);
        NoteObject noteObj = spawnedObj.GetComponent<NoteObject>();
        
        if (noteObj != null)
        {
            noteObj.targetHole = data.holeLaneIndex;
            noteObj.requiredState = data.actionType;
            noteObj.duration = data.holdLength;
            activeNotes.Add(noteObj);
        }
    }

    private void HandlePlayerInput()
    {
        // Register key down to handle one-time tap scoring and play error sounds
        if (Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.Mouse1))
        {
            CheckHit(true);
        }
        // Also support continuous hold notes/taps if player is actively holding and matching
        else if (playerController.IsPlayingNote)
        {
            CheckHit(false);
        }
    }

    private void CheckHit(bool isInitialClick)
    {
        int playerHole = playerController.GetCurrentHole();
        PlayerController.BreathState playerState = playerController.CurrentBreathState;

        NoteObject bestTarget = null;
        float smallestOffset = float.MaxValue;

        // Find the closest active note in the player's current lane requiring the current breath state
        for (int i = 0; i < activeNotes.Count; i++)
        {
            NoteObject note = activeNotes[i];
            if (note == null || note.IsCleared) continue;

            if (note.targetHole == playerHole && note.requiredState == playerState)
            {
                float offset = Mathf.Abs(note.transform.position.y - (-4.0f));
                if (offset <= 0.60f && offset < smallestOffset) // 0.60f is the OK Range
                {
                    smallestOffset = offset;
                    bestTarget = note;
                }
            }
        }

        if (bestTarget != null)
        {
            // We have a hit! Calculate accuracy rating
            bestTarget.IsCleared = true;
            activeNotes.Remove(bestTarget);

            RegisterHitScore(smallestOffset);
            PlayHarmonicaNote(playerHole - 1);

            Destroy(bestTarget.gameObject);
        }
        else if (isInitialClick)
        {
            // If the player clicked but didn't hit anything, play the mismatch rustle sound
            PlaySpitAirRustle();
        }
    }

    private void RegisterHitScore(float offset)
    {
        // Accuracy limits: Perfect (0.15), Good (0.35), OK (0.60)
        if (offset <= 0.15f)
        {
            perfectCount++;
            score += 100;
            combo++;
            Debug.Log("PERFECT! Offset: " + offset);
        }
        else if (offset <= 0.35f)
        {
            goodCount++;
            score += 50;
            combo++;
            Debug.Log("GOOD! Offset: " + offset);
        }
        else
        {
            okCount++;
            score += 25;
            combo++;
            Debug.Log("OK! Offset: " + offset);
        }

        if (combo > maxCombo)
        {
            maxCombo = combo;
        }
    }

    private void HandleNoteMissed(NoteObject note)
    {
        if (activeNotes.Contains(note))
        {
            activeNotes.Remove(note);
        }

        missCount++;
        combo = 0; // Reset combo on miss
        Debug.Log("MISS! Lane: " + note.targetHole);
    }

    private void PlayHarmonicaNote(int index)
    {
        if (audioSource == null) return;

        if (harmonicaNotes != null && index >= 0 && index < harmonicaNotes.Length)
        {
            AudioClip clip = harmonicaNotes[index];
            if (clip != null)
            {
                audioSource.PlayOneShot(clip);
            }
        }
    }

    private void PlaySpitAirRustle()
    {
        if (audioSource == null || spitAirRustle == null) return;
        audioSource.PlayOneShot(spitAirRustle);
    }
}

### 1. `RhythmManager.cs` Highlights
The script (written to `Assets/Scripts/RhythmManager.cs`) executes the entire game lifecycle cleanly:
*   **Decoupled Events:** Subscribes to `NoteObject.OnAnyNoteMissed` to automatically process misses and break player combos, avoiding expensive find loops.
*   **Coordinate Translation:** Translates the chart's lane indices `1` through `10` straight to global world positions on the horizontal plane using the exact blueprint formula (`X = laneIndex - 5.5f`).
*   **Timing Accuracy Mapping:** Measures real-time vertical displacement offsets from `Y = -4.0f` to score notes using the spec ranges:
    *   **Perfect:** `+/- 0.15` units
    *   **Good:** `+/- 0.35` units
    *   **OK:** `+/- 0.60` units
*   **Audio Routing Engine:** Plays corresponding harmonica scale audio clips `0` to `9` for valid hits, or the mismatch error clip `spit_air_rustle.wav` upon any bad clicks.

### 2. Guide to Set Up Option A (Dedicated Prefabs)

Since we are proceeding with **Option A (Dedicated Prefabs)**, follow these steps to configure them in the editor:

1.  **Create "Note_Blow" Prefab (Blue):**
    *   Create a 2D Sprite GameObject named `Note_Blow` (e.g. using a rounded square or circle sprite asset).
    *   Set its **SpriteRenderer** color to Neon Blue (`#1E90FF`).
    *   Attach the `NoteObject` component to it.
    *   In the inspector for `NoteObject`, set `Required State` to `Blow` and `Fall Speed` to `4.0`.
    *   Drag this GameObject into your Project assets folder to save it as a Prefab, and delete it from the Hierarchy.
2.  **Create "Note_Draw" Prefab (Amber):**
    *   Create a second 2D Sprite GameObject named `Note_Draw`.
    *   Set its **SpriteRenderer** color to Warm Amber (`#FF8C00`).
    *   Attach the `NoteObject` component to it.
    *   In the inspector for `NoteObject`, set `Required State` to `Draw` and `Fall Speed` to `4.0`.
    *   Drag this GameObject into your Project assets folder to save it as a Prefab, and delete it from the Hierarchy.
3.  **Link to the Rhythm Manager:**
    *   Attach the compiled `RhythmManager` script to a new GameObject named `RhythmManager`.
    *   Assign the `Note_Blow` prefab to the **Note Blow Prefab** field.
    *   Assign the `Note_Draw` prefab to the **Note Draw Prefab** field.

", "agent": "CoreAgent"}</THOUGHT><TOOL_CALL>{"tool_id": "Unity.WriteFile", "call_id": "cce13b54-a87f-4552-904d-ffa5fe9a3ef1", "args": {"createDirectories": true, "file_path": "Assets/Scripts/RhythmManager.cs", "content": "using System.Collections.Generic;
using UnityEngine;

public class RhythmManager : MonoBehaviour
{
    [Header("References")]
    [SerializeField] private PlayerController playerController;
    [SerializeField] private SongChart activeChart;
    
    [Header("Note Prefabs")]
    [SerializeField] private GameObject noteBlowPrefab; // Blue (Blow) note prefab
    [SerializeField] private GameObject noteDrawPrefab; // Amber (Draw) note prefab

    [Header("Audio Settings")]
    [SerializeField] private AudioSource audioSource;
    [SerializeField] private AudioClip[] harmonicaNotes; // Index 0-9 for Holes 1-10
    [SerializeField] private AudioClip spitAirRustle;    // Mismatched error sound

    [Header("Scoring & Combo")]
    [SerializeField] private int score = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int combo = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int maxCombo = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int perfectCount = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int goodCount = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int okCount = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int missCount = 0;

    private List<NoteObject> activeNotes = new List<NoteObject>();
    private int nextNoteIndex = 0;
    private float songTimer = 0f;
    private bool isSongPlaying = false;

    // Public getters for UI
    public int Score => score;
    public int Combo => combo;
    public int MaxCombo => maxCombo;
    public int PerfectCount => perfectCount;
    public int GoodCount => goodCount;
    public int OkCount => okCount;
    public int MissCount => missCount;
    public float SongTimer => songTimer;
    public bool IsSongPlaying => isSongPlaying;

    private void OnEnable()
    {
        NoteObject.OnAnyNoteMissed += HandleNoteMissed;
    }

    private void OnDisable()
    {
        NoteObject.OnAnyNoteMissed -= HandleNoteMissed;
    }

    public void StartSong()
    {
        if (activeChart == null)
        {
            Debug.LogError("Cannot start song: No SongChart assigned!");
            return;
        }

        // Sort chart timeline by spawnTimeOffset just in case
        activeChart.trackTimeline.Sort((a, b) => a.spawnTimeOffset.CompareTo(b.spawnTimeOffset));

        songTimer = 0f;
        nextNoteIndex = 0;
        activeNotes.Clear();
        score = 0;
        combo = 0;
        maxCombo = 0;
        perfectCount = 0;
        goodCount = 0;
        okCount = 0;
        missCount = 0;
        isSongPlaying = true;
    }

    private void Update()
    {
        if (!isSongPlaying) return;

        songTimer += Time.deltaTime;

        // 1. Spawning logic
        SpawnNotesInTimeline();

        // 2. Player Input / Hit Window logic
        HandlePlayerInput();
    }

    private void SpawnNotesInTimeline()
    {
        while (nextNoteIndex < activeChart.trackTimeline.Count &&
               songTimer >= activeChart.trackTimeline[nextNoteIndex].spawnTimeOffset)
        {
            NoteData noteData = activeChart.trackTimeline[nextNoteIndex];
            SpawnNote(noteData);
            nextNoteIndex++;
        }
    }

    private void SpawnNote(NoteData data)
    {
        // Calculate world X coordinate based on hole index
        // Hole 1 is at -4.5f, Hole 10 is at 4.5f.
        float spawnX = data.holeLaneIndex - 5.5f;
        Vector3 spawnPosition = new Vector3(spawnX, 6.0f, 0f);

        // Select prefab based on breath state
        GameObject prefabToSpawn = (data.actionType == PlayerController.BreathState.Blow) ? noteBlowPrefab : noteDrawPrefab;
        if (prefabToSpawn == null)
        {
            Debug.LogError($"Note prefab is missing for state: {data.actionType}");
            return;
        }

        GameObject spawnedObj = Instantiate(prefabToSpawn, spawnPosition, Quaternion.identity);
        NoteObject noteObj = spawnedObj.GetComponent<NoteObject>();
        
        if (noteObj != null)
        {
            noteObj.targetHole = data.holeLaneIndex;
            noteObj.requiredState = data.actionType;
            noteObj.duration = data.holdLength;
            activeNotes.Add(noteObj);
        }
    }

    private void HandlePlayerInput()
    {
        // Register key down to handle tap scoring and play error sounds on click mismatch
        if (Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.Mouse1))
        {
            CheckHit(true);
        }
        // Also support continuous holding for hold notes if player is actively holding and matching
        else if (playerController.IsPlayingNote)
        {
            CheckHit(false);
        }
    }

    private void CheckHit(bool isInitialClick)
    {
        int playerHole = playerController.GetCurrentHole();
        PlayerController.BreathState playerState = playerController.CurrentBreathState;

        NoteObject bestTarget = null;
        float smallestOffset = float.MaxValue;

        // Find the closest active note in the player's current lane requiring the current breath state
        for (int i = 0; i < activeNotes.Count; i++)
        {
            NoteObject note = activeNotes[i];
            if (note == null || note.IsCleared) continue;

            if (note.targetHole == playerHole && note.requiredState == playerState)
            {
                // Target hit zone is at Y = -4.0f
                float offset = Mathf.Abs(note.transform.position.y - (-4.0f));
                
                // Max OK range is +/- 0.60 units offset
                if (offset <= 0.60f && offset < smallestOffset)
                {
                    // If it is NOT the initial click (meaning player is holding RMB down),
                    // only allow hitting hold notes (duration > 0). Tap notes (duration == 0)
                    // MUST be cleared with an initial key down (GetKeyDown) to prevent cheesy holding.
                    if (!isInitialClick && note.duration == 0f)
                    {
                        continue;
                    }

                    smallestOffset = offset;
                    bestTarget = note;
                }
            }
        }

        if (bestTarget != null)
        {
            // We have a hit! Calculate accuracy rating
            bestTarget.IsCleared = true;
            activeNotes.Remove(bestTarget);

            RegisterHitScore(smallestOffset);
            PlayHarmonicaNote(playerHole - 1);

            Destroy(bestTarget.gameObject);
        }
        else if (isInitialClick)
        {
            // If the player clicked but didn't hit any valid note, play the mismatch rustle sound
            PlaySpitAirRustle();
        }
    }

    private void RegisterHitScore(float offset)
    {
        // Accuracy limits: Perfect (0.15), Good (0.35), OK (0.60)
        if (offset <= 0.15f)
        {
            perfectCount++;
            score += 100;
            combo++;
            Debug.Log("PERFECT! Offset: " + offset);
        }
        else if (offset <= 0.35f)
        {
            goodCount++;
            score += 50;
            combo++;
            Debug.Log("GOOD! Offset: " + offset);
        }
        else
        {
            okCount++;
            score += 25;
            combo++;
            Debug.Log("OK! Offset: " + offset);
        }

        if (combo > maxCombo)
        {
            maxCombo = combo;
        }
    }

    private void HandleNoteMissed(NoteObject note)
    {
        if (activeNotes.Contains(note))
        {
            activeNotes.Remove(note);
        }

        missCount++;
        combo = 0; // Reset combo on miss
        Debug.Log("MISS! Lane: " + note.targetHole);
    }

    private void PlayHarmonicaNote(int index)
    {
        if (audioSource == null) return;

        if (harmonicaNotes != null && index >= 0 && index < harmonicaNotes.Length)
        {
            AudioClip clip = harmonicaNotes[index];
            if (clip != null)
            {
                audioSource.PlayOneShot(clip);
            }
        }
    }

    private void PlaySpitAirRustle()
    {
        if (audioSource == null || spitAirRustle == null) return;
        audioSource.PlayOneShot(spitAirRustle);
    }
}

### 1. `RhythmManager.cs` Architecture

The script sits at `Assets/Scripts/RhythmManager.cs` and coordinates the central gameplay flow. It features:
*   **Time-Tracking Loop:** Monitors elapsed seconds (`songTimer += Time.deltaTime`).
*   **Sequential Spawn Queue:** Steps through the sorted `SongChart` timeline and instantiates the correct note prefab (`noteBlowPrefab` for Blue, `noteDrawPrefab` for Amber) as soon as `songTimer >= spawnTimeOffset`.
*   **Perfect Spatial Lane Translation:** Spawns notes offscreen at `Y = 6.0f` using the exact coordinate calculation from the specs:
    $$	ext{World X} = 	ext{laneIndex} - 5.5f$$
*   **Decoupled Miss Catching:** Hooks up to the static event `NoteObject.OnAnyNoteMissed` to cleanly increment the miss counter, reset the combo to `0`, and update tracking logs without high-overhead searches.

---

### 2. Hit Detection & RMB Holding Logic

The hit detection implementation accommodates both **instantiated clicks** and **continuous RMB hold controls** using a robust strategy:

```csharp
    private void HandlePlayerInput()
    {
        // Register key down to handle tap scoring and play error sounds on click mismatch
        if (Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.Mouse1))
        {
            CheckHit(true);
        }
        // Also support continuous holding for hold notes if player is actively holding and matching
        else if (playerController.IsPlayingNote)
        {
            CheckHit(false);
        }
    }
```

*   **Closest Target Search:** Filters active notes matching the player's current lane (determined by `playerController.GetCurrentHole()`) and active breath state (`playerController.CurrentBreathState`).
*   **Anti-Cheesy Hold Filter:** 
    *   If the user hits RMB on the initial frame (`isInitialClick == true`), they can hit either **Tap Notes** or **Sustain Hold Notes**.
    *   If the user holds down RMB continuously over subsequent frames (`isInitialClick == false`), they can **only** hit **Sustain Hold Notes** (`duration > 0f`). This prevents players from cheating standard tap notes by simply keeping the button pressed.
*   **Precision Offset Scoring (Relative to $Y = -4.0f$):**
    *   **Perfect Range:** $\le 0.15$ units $ightarrow$ $+100$ pts, combo up.
    *   **Good Range:** $\le 0.35$ units $ightarrow$ $+50$ pts, combo up.
    *   **OK Range:** $\le 0.60$ units $ightarrow$ $+25$ pts, combo up.
    *   **Out of bounds / Misaligned state:** Play the rustle audio error.

    *   `RhythmManager` queries `playerController.IsPlayingNote` to check the continuous holding status of the mouse button.

public class AudioManager : MonoBehaviour
{
    public static AudioManager Instance { get; private set; }

    [Header("Audio Sources")]
    [SerializeField] private AudioSource musicSource;
    [SerializeField] private AudioSource sfxSource;

    [Header("Audio Clips")]
    [SerializeField] private AudioClip backgroundMusic;
    [SerializeField] private AudioClip[] harmonicaNotes = new AudioClip[10]; // Indices 0-9 for Holes 1-10
    [SerializeField] private AudioClip spitAirRustle;                      // Error sound

    private void Awake()
    {
        if (Instance == null)
        {
            Instance = this;
        }
        else
        {
            Destroy(gameObject);
        }
    }

    public void PlayMusic()
    {
        if (musicSource != null && backgroundMusic != null)
        {
            musicSource.clip = backgroundMusic;
            musicSource.loop = true;
            musicSource.Play();
        }
    }

    public void StopMusic()
    {
        if (musicSource != null)
        {
            musicSource.Stop();
        }
    }

    public void PlayHarmonicaNote(int index)
    {
        if (sfxSource == null) return;

        if (index >= 0 && index < harmonicaNotes.Length)
        {
            AudioClip clip = harmonicaNotes[index];
            if (clip != null)
            {
                sfxSource.PlayOneShot(clip);
            }
        }
    }

    public void PlayErrorSound()
    {
        if (sfxSource != null && spitAirRustle != null)
        {
            sfxSource.PlayOneShot(spitAirRustle);
        }
    }
}

{
    [Header("References")]
    [SerializeField] private PlayerController playerController;
    [SerializeField] private RhythmManager rhythmManager;

    [Header("Oxygen UI")]
    [SerializeField] private UnityEngine.UI.Slider oxygenSlider;
    [SerializeField] private UnityEngine.UI.Image oxygenFillImage;
    [SerializeField] private TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI stunText; // Optional indicator

    [Header("Breath State UI")]
    [SerializeField] private UnityEngine.UI.Image statePanelImage;
    [SerializeField] private TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI stateText;

    [Header("Score & Combo UI")]
    [SerializeField] private TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI scoreText;
    [SerializeField] private TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI comboText;

    private readonly Color blowColor = new Color(0.117f, 0.564f, 1.0f); // #1E90FF
    private readonly Color drawColor = new Color(1.0f, 0.549f, 0.0f);   // #FF8C00
    private readonly Color stunColor = Color.red;

    void Update()
    {
        UpdateOxygenUI();
        UpdateBreathStateUI();
        UpdateScoreComboUI();
    }

    private void UpdateOxygenUI()
    {
        if (playerController == null) return;

        if (oxygenSlider != null)
        {
            oxygenSlider.value = playerController.OxygenLevel;
        }

        bool isStunned = playerController.IsStunned;

        if (stunText != null)
        {
            stunText.gameObject.SetActive(isStunned);
            if (isStunned)
            {
                stunText.text = $"STUNNED! ({playerController.StunTimer:F1}s)";
            }
        }

        if (oxygenFillImage != null)
        {
            oxygenFillImage.color = isStunned ? stunColor : Color.Lerp(Color.yellow, Color.green, 1.0f - Mathf.Abs(playerController.OxygenLevel - 0.5f) * 2f);
        }
    }

    private void UpdateBreathStateUI()
    {
        if (playerController == null) return;

        PlayerController.BreathState state = playerController.CurrentBreathState;

        if (statePanelImage != null)
        {
            statePanelImage.color = (state == PlayerController.BreathState.Blow) ? blowColor : drawColor;
        }

        if (stateText != null)
        {
            if (playerController.IsStunned)
            {
                stateText.text = "STUNNED";
            }
            else
            {
                stateText.text = (state == PlayerController.BreathState.Blow) ? "BLOW" : "DRAW";
            }
        }
    }

    private void UpdateScoreComboUI()
    {
        if (rhythmManager == null) return;

        if (scoreText != null)
        {
            scoreText.text = $"Score: {rhythmManager.Score}";
        }

        if (comboText != null)
        {
            comboText.text = rhythmManager.Combo > 0 ? $"Combo: {rhythmManager.Combo}" : "";
        }
    }
}

using UnityEngine;

public class RhythmManager : MonoBehaviour
{
    [Header("References")]
    [SerializeField] private PlayerController playerController;
    [SerializeField] private SongChart activeChart;
    
    [Header("Note Prefabs")]
    [SerializeField] private GameObject noteBlowPrefab; // Blue (Blow) note prefab
    [SerializeField] private GameObject noteDrawPrefab; // Amber (Draw) note prefab

    [Header("Scoring & Combo")]
    [SerializeField] private int score = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int combo = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int maxCombo = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int perfectCount = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int goodCount = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int okCount = 0;
    [SerializeField] private int missCount = 0;

    private List<NoteObject> activeNotes = new List<NoteObject>();
    private int nextNoteIndex = 0;
    private float songTimer = 0f;
    private bool isSongPlaying = false;

    // Public getters for UI
    public int Score => score;
    public int Combo => combo;
    public int MaxCombo => maxCombo;
    public int PerfectCount => perfectCount;
    public int GoodCount => goodCount;
    public int OkCount => okCount;
    public int MissCount => missCount;
    public float SongTimer => songTimer;
    public bool IsSongPlaying => isSongPlaying;

    private void OnEnable()
    {
        NoteObject.OnAnyNoteMissed += HandleNoteMissed;
    }

    private void OnDisable()
    {
        NoteObject.OnAnyNoteMissed -= HandleNoteMissed;
    }

    public void StartSong()
    {
        if (activeChart == null)
        {
            Debug.LogError("Cannot start song: No SongChart assigned!");
            return;
        }

        // Sort chart timeline by spawnTimeOffset just in case
        activeChart.trackTimeline.Sort((a, b) => a.spawnTimeOffset.CompareTo(b.spawnTimeOffset));

        songTimer = 0f;
        nextNoteIndex = 0;
        activeNotes.Clear();
        score = 0;
        combo = 0;
        maxCombo = 0;
        perfectCount = 0;
        goodCount = 0;
        okCount = 0;
        missCount = 0;
        isSongPlaying = true;

        if (AudioManager.Instance != null)
        {
            AudioManager.Instance.PlayMusic();
        }
    }

    private void Update()
    {
        if (!isSongPlaying) return;

        songTimer += Time.deltaTime;

        // 1. Spawning logic
        SpawnNotesInTimeline();

        // 2. Player Input / Hit Window logic
        HandlePlayerInput();
    }

    private void SpawnNotesInTimeline()
    {
        while (nextNoteIndex < activeChart.trackTimeline.Count &&
               songTimer >= activeChart.trackTimeline[nextNoteIndex].spawnTimeOffset)
        {
            NoteData noteData = activeChart.trackTimeline[nextNoteIndex];
            SpawnNote(noteData);
            nextNoteIndex++;
        }
    }

    private void SpawnNote(NoteData data)
    {
        // Calculate world X coordinate based on hole index
        // Hole 1 is at -4.5f, Hole 10 is at 4.5f.
        float spawnX = data.holeLaneIndex - 5.5f;
        Vector3 spawnPosition = new Vector3(spawnX, 6.0f, 0f);

        // Select prefab based on breath state
        GameObject prefabToSpawn = (data.actionType == PlayerController.BreathState.Blow) ? noteBlowPrefab : noteDrawPrefab;
        if (prefabToSpawn == null)
        {
            Debug.LogError($"Note prefab is missing for state: {data.actionType}");
            return;
        }

        GameObject spawnedObj = Instantiate(prefabToSpawn, spawnPosition, Quaternion.identity);
        NoteObject noteObj = spawnedObj.GetComponent<NoteObject>();
        
        if (noteObj != null)
        {
            noteObj.targetHole = data.holeLaneIndex;
            noteObj.requiredState = data.actionType;
            noteObj.duration = data.holdLength;
            activeNotes.Add(noteObj);
        }
    }

    private void HandlePlayerInput()
    {
        // Register key down to handle tap scoring and play error sounds on click mismatch
        if (Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.Mouse1))
        {
            CheckHit(true);
        }
        // Also support continuous holding for hold notes if player is actively holding and matching
        else if (playerController.IsPlayingNote)
        {
            CheckHit(false);
        }
    }

    private void CheckHit(bool isInitialClick)
    {
        int playerHole = playerController.GetCurrentHole();
        PlayerController.BreathState playerState = playerController.CurrentBreathState;

        NoteObject bestTarget = null;
        float smallestOffset = float.MaxValue;

        // Find the closest active note in the player's current lane requiring the current breath state
        for (int i = 0; i < activeNotes.Count; i++)
        {
            NoteObject note = activeNotes[i];
            if (note == null || note.IsCleared) continue;

            if (note.targetHole == playerHole && note.requiredState == playerState)
            {
                // Target hit zone is at Y = -4.0f
                float offset = Mathf.Abs(note.transform.position.y - (-4.0f));
                
                // Max OK range is +/- 0.60 units offset
                if (offset <= 0.60f && offset < smallestOffset)
                {
                    // If it is NOT the initial click (meaning player is holding RMB down),
                    // only allow hitting hold notes (duration > 0). Tap notes (duration == 0)
                    // MUST be cleared with an initial key down (GetKeyDown) to prevent cheesy holding.
                    if (!isInitialClick && note.duration == 0f)
                    {
                        continue;
                    }

                    smallestOffset = offset;
                    bestTarget = note;
                }
            }
        }

        if (bestTarget != null)
        {
            // We have a hit! Calculate accuracy rating
            bestTarget.IsCleared = true;
            activeNotes.Remove(bestTarget);

            RegisterHitScore(smallestOffset);

            if (AudioManager.Instance != null)
            {
                AudioManager.Instance.PlayHarmonicaNote(playerHole - 1);
            }

            Destroy(bestTarget.gameObject);
        }
        else if (isInitialClick)
        {
            // If the player clicked but didn't hit any valid note, play the mismatch rustle sound
            if (AudioManager.Instance != null)
            {
                AudioManager.Instance.PlayErrorSound();
            }
        }
    }

    private void RegisterHitScore(float offset)
    {
        // Accuracy limits: Perfect (0.15), Good (0.35), OK (0.60)
        if (offset <= 0.15f)
        {
            perfectCount++;
            score += 100;
            combo++;
            Debug.Log("PERFECT! Offset: " + offset);
        }
        else if (offset <= 0.35f)
        {
            goodCount++;
            score += 50;
            combo++;
            Debug.Log("GOOD! Offset: " + offset);
        }
        else
        {
            okCount++;
            score += 25;
            combo++;
            Debug.Log("OK! Offset: " + offset);
        }

        if (combo > maxCombo)
        {
            maxCombo = combo;
        }
    }

    private void HandleNoteMissed(NoteObject note)
    {
        if (activeNotes.Contains(note))
        {
            activeNotes.Remove(note);
        }

        missCount++;
        combo = 0; // Reset combo on miss
        Debug.Log("MISS! Lane: " + note.targetHole);
    }
}

        stunIndicatorText.text = "STUNNED!";
        stunIndicatorText.alignment = TMPro.TextAlignmentOptions.Center;
        stunIndicatorText.fontSize = 45;
        stunIndicatorText.color = Color.red;
        stunIndicatorText.gameObject.SetActive(false);
        RectTransform stunRT = stunTextObj.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
        stunRT.anchorMin = new Vector2(0.5f, 0.5f);
        stunRT.anchorMax = new Vector2(0.5f, 0.5f);
        stunRT.pivot = new Vector2(0.5f, 0.5f);
        stunRT.anchoredPosition = new Vector3(0, 150f, 0);
        stunRT.sizeDelta = new Vector2(500, 80);

        // 9. Game UI Manager Creation
        GameObject uiManagerObj = new GameObject("GameUIManager");
        var uiManager = uiManagerObj.AddComponent<GameUIManager>();
        result.RegisterObjectCreation(uiManagerObj);

        SerializedObject uiSO = new SerializedObject(uiManager);
        uiSO.FindProperty("playerController").objectReferenceValue = playerController;
        uiSO.FindProperty("rhythmManager").objectReferenceValue = rhythmManager;
        uiSO.FindProperty("oxygenSlider").objectReferenceValue = slider;
        uiSO.FindProperty("oxygenFillImage").objectReferenceValue = fillImage;
        uiSO.FindProperty("stunText").objectReferenceValue = stunIndicatorText;
        uiSO.FindProperty("statePanelImage").objectReferenceValue = breathImage;
        uiSO.FindProperty("stateText").objectReferenceValue = breathText;
        uiSO.FindProperty("scoreText").objectReferenceValue = scoreText;
        uiSO.FindProperty("comboText").objectReferenceValue = comboText;
        uiSO.ApplyModifiedProperties();

        // 10. Generate 10 Virtual Harmonica Lane Guidelines in the Scene
        GameObject linesContainer = new GameObject("LaneGuidelines");
        linesContainer.transform.position = Vector3.zero;
        
        for (int i = 1; i <= 10; i++)
        {
            float lineX = i - 5.5f;
            GameObject lineObj = new GameObject($"Lane_{i}_Line");
            lineObj.transform.parent = linesContainer.transform;
            lineObj.transform.position = new Vector3(lineX, 0, 1f); // Placed slightly behind notes

            var lineSr = lineObj.AddComponent<SpriteRenderer>();
            lineSr.sprite = AssetDatabase.GetBuiltInExtraResource<Sprite>("UI/Skin/Background.psd");
            lineSr.color = new Color(1f, 1f, 1f, 0.15f); // Very faint guideline
            lineObj.transform.localScale = new Vector3(0.08f, 10.0f, 1f); // Tall thin vertical line
        }

        // Add visual target baseline at Y = -4.0f
        GameObject targetLineObj = new GameObject("TargetHitZoneLine");
        targetLineObj.transform.position = new Vector3(0f, -4.0f, 0.5f);
        var targetSr = targetLineObj.AddComponent<SpriteRenderer>();
        targetSr.sprite = AssetDatabase.GetBuiltInExtraResource<Sprite>("UI/Skin/Background.psd");
        targetSr.color = new Color(0.2f, 0.8f, 0.2f, 0.5f); // Vibrant light green target bar
        targetLineObj.transform.localScale = new Vector3(10.0f, 0.15f, 1f); // Flat horizontal bar spanning lanes

        // Save the Scene
        UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.SaveScene(scene, scenePath);
        result.Log("MainScene built successfully with Camera, Player, RhythmManager, AudioManager, UI Canvas, and procedural Lane guidelines!");
    }
}

            for (int y = 0; y < tex.height; y++)
            {
                for (int x = 0; x < tex.width; x++)
                {
                    tex.SetPixel(x, y, Color.white);
                }
            }
            tex.Apply();
            byte[] pngBytes = tex.EncodeToPNG();
            File.WriteAllBytes(texturePath, pngBytes);
            AssetDatabase.Refresh();

            TextureImporter importer = AssetImporter.GetAtPath(texturePath) as TextureImporter;
            if (importer != null)

                importer.textureType = TextureImporterType.Sprite;
                importer.SaveAndReimport();
            }
        }

        // Load procedural Sprite asset

        if (solidSprite == null)
        {
            result.LogError("Failed to load procedural sprite!");
            return;
        }

        string scenePath = "Assets/Scenes/MainScene.unity";
        var scene = UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.NewScene(

        );
        
        // Find existing default Main Camera or create one
        GameObject cameraObj = GameObject.Find("Main Camera");
        if (cameraObj == null)
        {
            cameraObj = new GameObject("Main Camera");
            cameraObj.AddComponent<Camera>();
        }
        
        Camera mainCam = cameraObj.GetComponent<Camera>();
        mainCam.orthographic = true;
        mainCam.orthographicSize = 5f;
        cameraObj.transform.position = new Vector3(0, 0, -10f);
        result.RegisterObjectModification(mainCam);
        result.RegisterObjectModification(cameraObj.transform);

        GameObject dirLight = GameObject.Find("Directional Light");
        if (dirLight != null)
        {
            result.DestroyObject(dirLight);

        var blowNote = noteBlowSource.AddComponent<NoteObject>();
        blowNote.requiredState = PlayerController.BreathState.Blow;
        blowNote.targetHole = 1;

        var drawNote = noteDrawSource.AddComponent<NoteObject>();
        drawNote.requiredState = PlayerController.BreathState.Draw;
        drawNote.targetHole = 1;

        reticleSr.sprite = solidSprite;

}

// Simple test helper to auto start song on click or start
public class PlayTestBootstrapper : MonoBehaviour
{
    private void Start()
    {
        var manager = FindObjectOfType<RhythmManager>();
        if (manager != null)
        {
            manager.StartSong();
            Debug.Log("Song Autostarted!");
        }
    }
}

// Simple test helper to auto start song on click or start
public class PlayTestBootstrapper : MonoBehaviour
{
    private void Start()

    {
        // 1. Silent import of TMPro Essential Resources to make sure texts render perfectly!
        #if UNITY_EDITOR
        try
        {
            // Call TMPro silent importer
            System.Type tmpWindowType = System.Type.GetType("TMPro.TMP_PackageResourceImporterWindow, Unity.TextMeshPro.Editor");
            if (tmpWindowType != null)
            {
                var method = tmpWindowType.GetMethod("ImportResourcesSilent", System.Reflection.BindingFlags.Static | System.Reflection.BindingFlags.Public | System.Reflection.BindingFlags.NonPublic);

                {
                    method.Invoke(null, null);

                }
            }
        }
        catch (System.Exception e)
        {
            result.LogWarning("Could not load TMPro Importer: " + e.Message);
        }
        #endif

        // 2. Adjust Camera to Solid Color Charcoal background for high-contrast visuals
        GameObject cameraObj = GameObject.Find("Main Camera");
        if (cameraObj != null)
        {
            Camera mainCam = cameraObj.GetComponent<Camera>();
            if (mainCam != null)
            {
                mainCam.clearFlags = CameraClearFlags.SolidColor;
                mainCam.backgroundColor = new Color(0.1f, 0.1f, 0.11f, 1.0f); // #1A1A1C (Dark Charcoal)
                result.RegisterObjectModification(mainCam);
            }
        }

        // Save the active scene to apply changes
        UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.SaveOpenScenes();
        result.Log("Camera clear flags updated to Solid Color and scene saved!");
    }
}

        string texturePath = "Assets/Textures/SolidWhite.png";
        
        Texture2D tex = new Texture2D(16, 16);
        for (int y = 0; y < tex.height; y++)
        {
            for (int x = 0; x < tex.width; x++)
            {
                tex.SetPixel(x, y, Color.white);
            }
        }
        tex.Apply();
        byte[] pngBytes = tex.EncodeToPNG();
        File.WriteAllBytes(texturePath, pngBytes);
        AssetDatabase.ImportAsset(texturePath, ImportAssetOptions.ForceUpdate);

        // Force convert import settings to Sprite (2D and UI)
        TextureImporter importer = AssetImporter.GetAtPath(texturePath) as TextureImporter;
        if (importer != null)
        {
            importer.textureType = TextureImporterType.Sprite;
            importer.spriteImportMode = SpriteImportMode.Single;
            importer.SaveAndReimport();
        }

        // Force load Sprite asset after reimporting
        Sprite solidSprite = AssetDatabase.LoadAssetAtPath<Sprite>(texturePath);
        if (solidSprite == null)
        {
            result.LogError("Failed to load procedural sprite asset!");
            return;
        }

        // 2. Open or Create Scene
        string scenePath = "Assets/Scenes/MainScene.unity";
        var scene = UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.NewScene(
            UnityEditor.SceneManagement.NewSceneSetup.DefaultGameObjects, 
            UnityEditor.SceneManagement.NewSceneMode.Single
        );
        
        // Find existing default Main Camera or create one
        GameObject cameraObj = GameObject.Find("Main Camera");
        if (cameraObj == null)
        {
            cameraObj = new GameObject("Main Camera");
            cameraObj.AddComponent<Camera>();
        }
        
        Camera mainCam = cameraObj.GetComponent<Camera>();
        mainCam.orthographic = true;
        mainCam.orthographicSize = 5f;
        mainCam.clearFlags = CameraClearFlags.SolidColor;
        mainCam.backgroundColor = new Color(0.1f, 0.1f, 0.11f, 1.0f); // Solid charcoal
        cameraObj.transform.position = new Vector3(0, 0, -10f);
        result.RegisterObjectModification(mainCam);
        result.RegisterObjectModification(cameraObj.transform);

        // Remove Directional Light for 2D UI game if it exists
        GameObject dirLight = GameObject.Find("Directional Light");
        if (dirLight != null)
        {
            result.DestroyObject(dirLight);
        }

        // 3. Create Note Prefabs procedurally using the sprite asset
        GameObject noteBlowSource = new GameObject("NoteBlow_Source");
        var blowSr = noteBlowSource.AddComponent<SpriteRenderer>();
        blowSr.sprite = solidSprite;
        blowSr.color = new Color(0.117f, 0.564f, 1.0f); // Neon Blue
        var blowNote = noteBlowSource.AddComponent<NoteObject>();
        blowNote.requiredState = PlayerController.BreathState.Blow;
        blowNote.targetHole = 1;
        blowNote.fallSpeed = 4f;

        GameObject noteDrawSource = new GameObject("NoteDraw_Source");
        var drawSr = noteDrawSource.AddComponent<SpriteRenderer>();
        drawSr.sprite = solidSprite;
        drawSr.color = new Color(1.0f, 0.549f, 0.0f); // Warm Amber
        var drawNote = noteDrawSource.AddComponent<NoteObject>();
        drawNote.requiredState = PlayerController.BreathState.Draw;
        drawNote.targetHole = 1;
        drawNote.fallSpeed = 4f;

        string blowPrefabPath = "Assets/Prefabs/NoteBlow.prefab";
        string drawPrefabPath = "Assets/Prefabs/NoteDraw.prefab";
        
        GameObject blowPrefab = PrefabUtility.SaveAsPrefabAsset(noteBlowSource, blowPrefabPath);
        GameObject drawPrefab = PrefabUtility.SaveAsPrefabAsset(noteDrawSource, drawPrefabPath);

        Object.DestroyImmediate(noteBlowSource);
        Object.DestroyImmediate(noteDrawSource);

        // 4. Create Player & Mouth Reticle
        GameObject playerObj = new GameObject("PlayerController");
        var playerController = playerObj.AddComponent<PlayerController>();
        result.RegisterObjectCreation(playerObj);

        GameObject reticleObj = new GameObject("MouthReticle");
        reticleObj.transform.parent = playerObj.transform;
        reticleObj.transform.position = new Vector3(0, -4.0f, 0);
        var reticleSr = reticleObj.AddComponent<SpriteRenderer>();
        reticleSr.sprite = solidSprite;
        reticleSr.color = Color.white;
        reticleObj.transform.localScale = new Vector3(1.0f, 0.4f, 1.0f); // Wide thin mouth organ shape

        // Assign field on PlayerController via SerializedObject
        SerializedObject playerSO = new SerializedObject(playerController);
        playerSO.FindProperty("mouthReticle").objectReferenceValue = reticleObj.transform;
        playerSO.ApplyModifiedProperties();

        // 5. Create Audio Manager
        GameObject audioManagerObj = new GameObject("AudioManager");
        var audioManager = audioManagerObj.AddComponent<AudioManager>();
        result.RegisterObjectCreation(audioManagerObj);

        GameObject musicSourceObj = new GameObject("MusicSource");
        musicSourceObj.transform.parent = audioManagerObj.transform;
        var musicSource = musicSourceObj.AddComponent<AudioSource>();
        
        GameObject sfxSourceObj = new GameObject("SFXSource");
        sfxSourceObj.transform.parent = audioManagerObj.transform;
        var sfxSource = sfxSourceObj.AddComponent<AudioSource>();

        SerializedObject audioSO = new SerializedObject(audioManager);
        audioSO.FindProperty("musicSource").objectReferenceValue = musicSource;
        audioSO.FindProperty("sfxSource").objectReferenceValue = sfxSource;
        audioSO.ApplyModifiedProperties();

        // 6. Create Song Chart
        SongChart chart = ScriptableObject.CreateInstance<SongChart>();
        chart.trackingTitle = "Mary Had a Little Lamb";
        chart.beatsPerMinute = 120f;
        chart.trackTimeline = new List<NoteData>
        {
            new NoteData { spawnTimeOffset = 1.0f, holeLaneIndex = 5, actionType = PlayerController.BreathState.Blow, holdLength = 0f },
            new NoteData { spawnTimeOffset = 2.0f, holeLaneIndex = 4, actionType = PlayerController.BreathState.Draw, holdLength = 0f },
            new NoteData { spawnTimeOffset = 3.0f, holeLaneIndex = 3, actionType = PlayerController.BreathState.Blow, holdLength = 0f },
            new NoteData { spawnTimeOffset = 4.0f, holeLaneIndex = 4, actionType = PlayerController.BreathState.Draw, holdLength = 0f },
            new NoteData { spawnTimeOffset = 5.0f, holeLaneIndex = 5, actionType = PlayerController.BreathState.Blow, holdLength = 0f },
            new NoteData { spawnTimeOffset = 6.0f, holeLaneIndex = 5, actionType = PlayerController.BreathState.Blow, holdLength = 0f },
            new NoteData { spawnTimeOffset = 7.0f, holeLaneIndex = 5, actionType = PlayerController.BreathState.Blow, holdLength = 1.0f }
        };
        AssetDatabase.CreateAsset(chart, "Assets/Charts/MaryHadALittleLamb.asset");
        AssetDatabase.SaveAssets();

        // 7. Create Rhythm Manager (NO TAG SETTINGS, purely field-linked)
        GameObject rhythmManagerObj = new GameObject("RhythmManager");
        var rhythmManager = rhythmManagerObj.AddComponent<RhythmManager>();
        result.RegisterObjectCreation(rhythmManagerObj);

        SerializedObject rhythmSO = new SerializedObject(rhythmManager);
        rhythmSO.FindProperty("playerController").objectReferenceValue = playerController;
        rhythmSO.FindProperty("activeChart").objectReferenceValue = chart;
        rhythmSO.FindProperty("noteBlowPrefab").objectReferenceValue = blowPrefab;
        rhythmSO.FindProperty("noteDrawPrefab").objectReferenceValue = drawPrefab;
        rhythmSO.ApplyModifiedProperties();

        // 8. Create UI Canvas Hierarchy
        GameObject canvasObj = new GameObject("Canvas");
        var canvas = canvasObj.AddComponent<Canvas>();
        canvas.renderMode = RenderMode.ScreenSpaceOverlay;
        
        var scaler = canvasObj.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.CanvasScaler>();
        scaler.uiScaleMode = UnityEngine.UI.CanvasScaler.ScaleMode.ScaleWithScreenSize;
        scaler.referenceResolution = new Vector2(1920, 1080);
        scaler.screenMatchMode = UnityEngine.UI.CanvasScaler.ScreenMatchMode.MatchWidthOrHeight;
        scaler.matchWidthOrHeight = 0.5f;

        canvasObj.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.GraphicRaycaster>();
        result.RegisterObjectCreation(canvasObj);

        // UI Event System
        GameObject eventSystemObj = new GameObject("EventSystem");
        eventSystemObj.AddComponent<UnityEngine.EventSystems.EventSystem>();
        eventSystemObj.AddComponent<UnityEngine.EventSystems.StandaloneInputModule>();
        result.RegisterObjectCreation(eventSystemObj);

        // Breath Panel
        GameObject breathPanel = new GameObject("BreathPanel");
        breathPanel.transform.parent = canvasObj.transform;
        var breathImage = breathPanel.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.Image>();
        breathImage.sprite = solidSprite;
        breathImage.type = UnityEngine.UI.Image.Type.Sliced;
        breathImage.color = new Color(0.117f, 0.564f, 1.0f);
        
        RectTransform panelRT = breathPanel.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
        panelRT.anchorMin = new Vector2(0.5f, 0.1f);

        breathText.text = "BLOW";
        breathText.alignment = TMPro.TextAlignmentOptions.Center;
        breathText.fontSize = 36;

        RectTransform textRT = breathTextObj.GetComponent<RectTransform>();

        // Oxygen Slider
        GameObject sliderObj = new GameObject("OxygenSlider");

        GameObject targetLineObj = new GameObject("TargetHitZoneLine");
        targetLineObj.transform.position = new Vector3(0f, -4.0f, 0.5f);
        var targetSr = targetLineObj.AddComponent<SpriteRenderer>();
        targetSr.sprite = solidSprite;
        targetSr.color = new Color(0.2f, 0.8f, 0.2f, 0.5f); // Vibrant light green target bar
        targetLineObj.transform.localScale = new Vector3(10.0f, 0.15f, 1f); // Flat horizontal bar spanning lanes

        // 11. Trigger Game Launch Test Setup on Start
        GameObject testSetupObj = new GameObject("PlayTestBootstrapper");
        var testScript = testSetupObj.AddComponent<PlayTestBootstrapper>();
        result.RegisterObjectCreation(testSetupObj);

        // Save the Scene
        UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.SaveScene(scene, scenePath);
        result.Log("MainScene built successfully with Camera, Player, RhythmManager, AudioManager, UI Canvas, and procedural Lane guidelines!");
    }
}

// Simple test helper to auto start song on click or start
public class PlayTestBootstrapper : MonoBehaviour
{
    private void Start()
    {
        var manager = FindObjectOfType<RhythmManager>();
        if (manager != null)
        {
            manager.StartSong();
            Debug.Log("Song Autostarted!");
        }
    }
}

I've pinpointed the NullReferenceException to TextMeshPro, specifically within the `MaterialReference` constructor. It seems to be triggered during the import of TMPro Essential Resources, indicating a potential issue with how these resources are being initialized.

", "agent": "CoreAgent"}</THOUGHT><THOUGHT>{"content": "**Exploring TextMeshPro Issues**

I'm considering if the default font asset isn't being registered correctly or is being accessed before full editor load. To circumvent TMPro initialization hiccups in a prototype, I'm contemplating switching to standard `UnityEngine.UI.Text`, a robust fallback, though I'll first investigate resolving the TMPro error directly.

", "agent": "CoreAgent"}</THOUGHT><THOUGHT>{"content": "**Resolving TextMeshPro Instantiation**

I've discovered that Unity 2022.3's TextMeshPro instantiates with a `NullReferenceException` if the TMPro Settings file is absent. The immediate solution involves procedurally loading and assigning the default font asset. I'll implement this fix before considering fallback options.

", "agent": "CoreAgent"}</THOUGHT><TOOL_CALL>{"tool_id": "Unity.RunCommand", "call_id": "2c9edb87-70a4-47dc-bdab-1f971fc546ce", "args": {"code": "using UnityEngine;
using UnityEditor;
using System.IO;
using System.Collections.Generic;

internal class CommandScript : IRunCommand
{
    public void Execute(ExecutionResult result)
    {
        // Find our Main Scene or use current
        string scenePath = "Assets/Scenes/MainScene.unity";
        var scene = UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.OpenScene(scenePath);

        // Find the imported LiberationSans SDF font asset
        TMPro.TMP_FontAsset defaultFont = AssetDatabase.LoadAssetAtPath<TMPro.TMP_FontAsset>("Assets/TextMesh Pro/Resources/Fonts & Materials/LiberationSans SDF.asset");
        if (defaultFont == null)
        {
            // Search globally for any SDF font asset in Project
            string[] guids = AssetDatabase.FindAssets("t:TMP_FontAsset");
            if (guids.Length > 0)
            {
                string path = AssetDatabase.GUIDToAssetPath(guids[0]);
                defaultFont = AssetDatabase.LoadAssetAtPath<TMPro.TMP_FontAsset>(path);
            }
        }

        if (defaultFont == null)
        {
            result.LogWarning("SDF default font not found! UI texts might not render properly until Unity editor processes imported assets.");
        }

        // Find and fix TMPro text objects
        var tmproTexts = Object.FindObjectsOfType<TMPro.TextMeshProUGUI>(true);
        foreach (var text in tmproTexts)
        {
            if (defaultFont != null)
            {
                text.font = defaultFont;
                EditorUtility.SetDirty(text);
            }
        }

        // Save the active scene to apply changes
        UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.SaveScene(scene, scenePath);
        result.Log($"Assigned default FontAsset to {tmproTexts.Length} TMPro text elements successfully!");
    }
}

I'm investigating why the Canvas elements aren't appearing. My current focus is on understanding the rendering pipeline, as I've confirmed the GameObject and its core components are correctly set up. The primary suspect is now the lack of a designated camera.

", "agent": "CoreAgent"}</THOUGHT><THOUGHT>{"content": "**Adjusting UI Hierarchy**

I've identified a key issue: parenting UI elements incorrectly within the Canvas. It seems setting the parent without `worldPositionStays = false` is causing unexpected scale and position problems. I'm now adjusting the code to use `transform.SetParent(parent, false)` for all child RectTransforms.

", "agent": "CoreAgent"}</THOUGHT><TOOL_CALL>{"tool_id": "Unity.RunCommand", "call_id": "ad24d575-bce6-4fa7-b8a8-e740ce9563a1", "args": {"title": "Reset RectTransform local scales and rotations", "code": "using UnityEngine;
using UnityEditor;

internal class CommandScript : IRunCommand
{
    public void Execute(ExecutionResult result)
    {
        string scenePath = "Assets/Scenes/MainScene.unity";
        var scene = UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.OpenScene(scenePath);

        GameObject canvasObj = GameObject.Find("Canvas");
        if (canvasObj == null)
        {
            result.LogError("Canvas not found!");
            return;
        }

        // Fix all children of the Canvas to have proper scale (1,1,1) and local position resets
        foreach (Transform child in canvasObj.transform)
        {
            RectTransform rt = child.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            if (rt != null)
            {
                // Reset local scale to (1, 1, 1)
                rt.localScale = Vector3.one;
                
                // Keep local rotation reset
                rt.localRotation = Quaternion.identity;

                result.Log($"Fixed RectTransform scale and rotation on {child.name}");
            }
        }

        // Let's also fix the nesting inside BreathPanel (the BreathText text element)
        GameObject breathPanel = GameObject.Find("BreathPanel");
        if (breathPanel != null)
        {
            foreach (Transform child in breathPanel.transform)
            {
                RectTransform rt = child.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
                if (rt != null)
                {
                    rt.localScale = Vector3.one;
                    rt.localRotation = Quaternion.identity;
                }
            }
        }

        // Let's also do the same for OxygenSlider parts
        GameObject sliderObj = GameObject.Find("OxygenSlider");
        if (sliderObj != null)
        {
            foreach (Transform child in sliderObj.transform)
            {
                RectTransform rt = child.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
                if (rt != null)
                {
                    rt.localScale = Vector3.one;
                    rt.localRotation = Quaternion.identity;
                    
                    // Nested children (like Fill Area or Background)
                    foreach (Transform nestedChild in child)
                    {
                        RectTransform nRT = nestedChild.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
                        if (nRT != null)
                        {
                            nRT.localScale = Vector3.one;
                            nRT.localRotation = Quaternion.identity;
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.SaveScene(scene, scenePath);
        result.Log("Nesting scale corrections applied and scene saved successfully!");
    }
}
"}, "agent": "CoreAgent"}</TOOL_CALL><TOOL_RESULT>{"tool_id": "Unity.RunCommand", "call_id": "ad24d575-bce6-4fa7-b8a8-e740ce9563a1", "result": using UnityEditor;

internal class CommandScript : IRunCommand
{
    public void Execute(ExecutionResult result)
    {
        string scenePath = "Assets/Scenes/MainScene.unity";
        var scene = UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.OpenScene(scenePath);

        // 1. Reset BreathText
        GameObject breathTextObj = GameObject.Find("BreathText");
        if (breathTextObj != null)
        {
            RectTransform rt = breathTextObj.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            if (rt != null)
            {
                rt.anchoredPosition = Vector2.zero;
                result.Log("Reset BreathText anchoredPosition to zero.");
            }
        }

        // 2. Reset Slider Background & Fill Area
        GameObject sliderObj = GameObject.Find("OxygenSlider");
        if (sliderObj != null)
        {
            foreach (Transform child in sliderObj.transform)
            {
                RectTransform rt = child.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
                if (rt != null)
                {
                    // If the child is Background, Fill Area, or Handle Slide Area, reset its position
                    if (child.name == "Background" || child.name == "Fill Area")
                    {
                        rt.anchoredPosition = Vector2.zero;
                        rt.sizeDelta = Vector2.zero; // Stretch
                        result.Log($"Reset OxygenSlider child {child.name} position to zero.");
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        // 3. Fix the StunText as well just in case
        GameObject stunTextObj = GameObject.Find("StunText");
        if (stunTextObj != null)
        {
            RectTransform rt = stunTextObj.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            if (rt != null)
            {
                rt.anchoredPosition = new Vector2(0f, 150f);
            }
        }

        UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.SaveScene(scene, scenePath);
        result.Log("Sub-elements positions fully corrected and scene saved!");
    }
}

using UnityEditor;

internal class CommandScript : IRunCommand
{
    public void Execute(ExecutionResult result)
    {
        string scenePath = "Assets/Scenes/MainScene.unity";
        var scene = UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.OpenScene(scenePath);

        // Find all UI Image components in the scene
        var images = Object.FindObjectsOfType<UnityEngine.UI.Image>(true);
        int changedCount = 0;
        foreach (var img in images)
        {
            if (img.type == UnityEngine.UI.Image.Type.Sliced)
            {
                img.type = UnityEngine.UI.Image.Type.Simple;
                EditorUtility.SetDirty(img);
                changedCount++;
            }
        }

        UnityEditor.SceneManagement.EditorSceneManager.SaveScene(scene, scenePath);
        result.Log($"Changed {changedCount} images from Sliced to Simple.");
    }
}
